name: Blanquette of Veal & Cauliflower
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the veal and 4 cups of broth in a 5-quart heavy pot over high heat on top of the stove. Cover and bring to a boil.
2. Remove any scum that forms on the surface, then add the remaining 2 cups broth, onion, celery, carrot, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and lemon.
3. Reduce heat to low and let simmer for 1 1/4 hours, or until the meat is just tender.
4. Meanwhile, melt the butter in a medium pot over low heat. Whisk in the flour. Cook, stirring for 2 minutes, then remove from heat. Set aside until the meat is cooked.
5. When the meat is done, using a slotted spoon, remove it from the liquid and discard any vegetables that cling to the veal.
6. Return the butter-flour mixture to medium-low heat on the stove and strain the cooking liquid into it, whisking vigorously. Discard the vegetables in the strainer.
7. Cook, stirring until the mixture thickens, about 3 to 5 minutes. Add cauliflower and continue to cook for an additional 15 minutes.
8. Skim off any residue that rises to the surface.
9. When ready to serve dinner, add the veal to the cauliflower and sauce, then heat through. Whisk 1/2 cup of hot sauce with the sour cream, then add this mixture to the stew.
10. Do not reheat the sauce after adding the sour cream. Pour the stew into a serving dish and accompany with rice pilaf or buttered noodles.
